title: Add pre-commit skill-sync hook
description: Update the root pre-commit config so it enforces `python3 tools/skills/sync_selected_skills.py --check` and matches the README.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] Pre-commit runs the selected-skill sync check | - [x] README and pre-commit behavior match

title: Expand PR workflow path coverage
description: Update the PR review workflow path filters so root docs such as `README.md` and `CONTRIBUTING.md` trigger validation and review.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] Root docs changes trigger the workflow | - [x] Workflow scope matches repo reality

title: Split validation from AI PR review
description: Create a dedicated validation workflow for push and pull_request status checks, leaving AI review as a separate PR-focused workflow.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] Deterministic validation runs on default-branch pushes | - [x] README badges and workflow purpose are clearer

title: Add contribution templates
description: Add lightweight GitHub pull request and issue templates that fit the repo's current informal contribution process.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] PR template exists | - [x] At least one issue template exists | - [x] Templates match the current contribution style

title: Fix Gemini action auth input
description: Update the Gemini CLI workflow step to use the correct `gemini_api_key` action input so the configured repository secret is actually passed through.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] Workflow uses the correct action input name | - [x] Gemini CLI can receive the configured repository secret

title: Replace Gemini action wrapper
description: Replace the unsupported Gemini GitHub Action wrapper usage with an explicit CLI run that reads the generated prompt file and posts the result to the PR separately.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] Workflow reads the generated prompt file directly | - [x] Gemini output is summarized and posted separately to the PR | - [x] Runner creates the Gemini state directory before invoking the CLI

title: Define stack doc and skill split
description: Document the chosen pattern: stack skills are the primary deep agent-facing source, `skills/<stack>/references/` holds specifics, stack agent files stay thin, and stack spec docs define project-template conventions while remaining standalone.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] Canonical responsibilities for docs, skills, and AGENTS files are documented | - [x] Required file layout and naming conventions are defined | - [x] Rollout constraints for standalone copied templates are explicit

title: Refactor Next.js stack to skill-first guidance
description: Create a single repo-owned `nextjs` skill with deep references, slim the Next.js agent instruction triplet into routing and always-hot guardrails, and reduce `specs/nextjs/docs/` to concise standalone project guidance.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] `skills/nextjs/SKILL.md` defines trigger rules, workflow, and targeted reference loading | - [x] `skills/nextjs/references/` covers the deep Next.js specifics needed for implementation and review | - [x] `specs/nextjs` agent files route to the skill and keep only minimal always-hot rules | - [x] `specs/nextjs/docs/` remains useful as a standalone, thinner template doc set

title: Refactor Python stack to skill-first guidance
description: Create a single repo-owned `python` skill with deep references, slim the Python agent instruction triplet into routing and always-hot guardrails, and reduce `specs/python/docs/` to concise standalone project guidance.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] `skills/python/SKILL.md` defines trigger rules, workflow, and targeted reference loading | - [x] `skills/python/references/` covers the deep Python specifics needed for implementation and review | - [x] `specs/python` agent files route to the skill and keep only minimal always-hot rules | - [x] `specs/python/docs/` remains useful as a standalone, thinner template doc set

title: Refactor TypeScript stack to skill-first guidance
description: Create a single repo-owned `typescript` skill with deep references, slim the TypeScript agent instruction triplet into routing and always-hot guardrails, and reduce `specs/typescript/docs/` to concise standalone project guidance.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] `skills/typescript/SKILL.md` defines trigger rules, workflow, and targeted reference loading | - [ ] `skills/typescript/references/` covers the deep TypeScript specifics needed for implementation and review | - [ ] `specs/typescript` agent files route to the skill and keep only minimal always-hot rules | - [ ] `specs/typescript/docs/` remains useful as a standalone, thinner template doc set

title: Refactor Power BI stack to skill-first guidance
description: Create a single repo-owned `powerbi` skill with deep references, slim the Power BI agent instruction triplet into routing and always-hot guardrails, and reduce `specs/powerbi/docs/` to concise standalone project guidance.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] `skills/powerbi/SKILL.md` defines trigger rules, workflow, and targeted reference loading | - [x] `skills/powerbi/references/` covers the deep Power BI specifics needed for implementation and review | - [x] `specs/powerbi` agent files route to the skill and keep only minimal always-hot rules | - [x] `specs/powerbi/docs/` remains useful as a standalone, thinner template doc set

title: Validate stack skill rollout and standalone templates
description: Validate the new stack skills, keep the selected-skill mirror intentionally limited to the chosen repo-local subset, and confirm each stack spec remains coherent and safe when copied without repo-local skills.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] Unselected repo-owned stack skills remain intentionally absent from `.agents/skills/`, and selected skills stay mirrored consistently with `.agents/skills/selected-skills.txt` | - [x] Agent file and template validators pass after the rollout | - [x] Each stack spec remains understandable and safe as a standalone template without repo-local skills

title: Define provider-agnostic scaffold policy
description: Document the follow-up template direction: `AGENTS.md` is the only checked-in canonical agent artifact, provider-specific rename or copy is optional user guidance, and specs must stay standalone without assuming package manager, repo shape, or deployment model beyond the stack itself.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] Policy states that `AGENTS.md` is the only required checked-in agent instruction file | - [x] Policy explains optional user-side copy or rename for provider-specific filenames | - [x] Policy says stack specs must avoid unnecessary assumptions about libraries, apps, or workspaces

title: Remove provider-specific agent files repo-wide
description: Delete checked-in `CLAUDE.md` and `GEMINI.md` files from the repository root and every spec, preserving any necessary shared guidance in `AGENTS.md` and adjacent docs.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] No checked-in `CLAUDE.md` or `GEMINI.md` files remain in the repo | - [x] Root and spec `AGENTS.md` files remain complete after removing duplicates | - [x] No template instructions require provider-specific duplicates to be present

title: Update repo docs, checks, and tooling for AGENTS-only templates
description: Rewrite repo docs, review prompts, validators, and agent-doc tooling so they no longer assume synced provider-specific files and instead enforce a single canonical `AGENTS.md` contract plus copy or rename guidance.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] Repo docs describe `AGENTS.md` as the sole checked-in canonical artifact | - [x] Checks and validators stop requiring `CLAUDE.md` and `GEMINI.md` | - [x] Agent-doc tooling and related docs no longer describe provider-file sync as the default pattern

title: Add explicit bootstrap and copy-rename guidance to shared templates
description: Update shared README guidance so copied scaffolds clearly mark starter defaults, tell users to replace placeholder commands and workflows before relying on them, and explain that `AGENTS.md` can be copied or renamed for tool-specific entrypoints if a destination repo wants that.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] Root and shared template docs make copy-after-edit steps explicit and hard to miss | - [x] Guidance explains optional copy or rename from `AGENTS.md` to provider-specific filenames in destination repos | - [x] Placeholder automation and starter defaults are clearly labeled as templates rather than working integrations

title: Generalize the TypeScript scaffold for any repo shape
description: Rework `specs/typescript` so its agent guidance, docs, starter manifest, checks, and workflows stay useful for apps, libraries, and workspaces without hardcoding `npm`, `eslint`, `vitest`, `Node 22`, or single-package assumptions as if they were universal truths.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] TypeScript template files avoid pretending one package manager, runner, or repo layout is universally correct | - [ ] Risky starter files clearly identify placeholder or starter-default values that must be reconciled after copy | - [ ] The stack remains standalone and useful without variants or repo-local skills

title: Generalize the Python scaffold for any repo shape
description: Review `specs/python` for false specificity and remove assumptions about package manager, environment tooling, test runner, layout, or deployment model so the scaffold works as a neutral Python starting point for apps, libraries, and workspaces.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Python template files avoid locking users into one tooling stack unless clearly labeled as starter defaults | - [ ] Commands, docs, and workflows stay aligned and explicitly customizable after copy | - [ ] The stack remains standalone and useful without variants or repo-local skills

title: Generalize the Next.js scaffold without single-shape assumptions
description: Review `specs/nextjs` for assumptions about repository layout, package manager, runtime, or deployment setup that go beyond Next.js itself, and tighten the template so it can fit different Next.js project arrangements without implying one exact project shape.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Next.js template guidance distinguishes framework requirements from optional starter choices | - [ ] Repo-shape and deployment assumptions are either removed or clearly labeled for user editing | - [ ] The copied template stays standalone and does not rely on hidden repo conventions

title: Generalize the Power BI scaffold without unnecessary assumptions
description: Review `specs/powerbi` for assumptions about workspace layout, publishing flow, governance setup, or automation readiness, and keep only the guidance that is broadly safe for a copied Power BI scaffold.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Power BI template guidance keeps only broadly safe defaults or clearly marked placeholders | - [ ] Publishing, governance, and automation instructions do not overstate what is configured out of the box | - [ ] The copied template stays standalone and easy to adapt

title: Validate provider-agnostic scaffold rollout
description: Run the repo validators and manually audit the copied-template experience after the AGENTS-only and project-shape-agnostic changes so the repo remains coherent, standalone, and free of stale references to removed provider files or false-specific starter assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Required validators pass after the cleanup | - [ ] No stale references to `CLAUDE.md` or `GEMINI.md` remain in docs, checks, or templates | - [ ] Each spec reads as a copy-ready standalone scaffold that does not assume one exact project shape

title: Disable AI PR review workflow
description: Disable the Gemini-backed pull request review workflow so PRs stop failing or burning limited API quota on clean changes, while keeping deterministic repo validation intact.
acceptance_criteria: - [x] AI PR review no longer runs automatically for this repository | - [x] Deterministic validation checks still run for pull requests | - [x] Repo docs or workflow comments do not imply the Gemini review is still active
